skill.name: specdd-trace
skill.description: Trace SpecDD specifications to code, tests, documentation, changed files, and coverage gaps. Identifies the chain of authority from bootstrap to local specs.
skill.summary: Trace spec-to-code relationships and identify missing links in SpecDD projects.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 9 patterns, all determined to be false positives after review. The 8 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are inline code references in Markdown documentation (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`) — standard Markdown backtick formatting, not executable code. The 1 'weak cryptographic algorithm' finding at line 3 is the YAML frontmatter description field and contains no cryptographic code. The skill is a 65-line Markdown instruction file with no executable code, no network access, and no data exfiltration patterns.

content.user_title: Trace Specs to Code in Your Project
content.value_statement: Developers struggle to understand how specifications connect to implementation. This skill traces the chain from bootstrap rules through local specs to code, tests, and docs, surfacing missing links and stale contracts.
actual_capabilities: Resolves the active bootstrap chain for a target SpecDD project
Traces specification authority from root to local specs
Identifies which code, tests, and docs satisfy each spec contract
Reports missing links between specs and implementation
Detects stale specs and untested scenarios
Maps changed files back to their governing specifications
limitations: Only works for projects that already have a .specdd/bootstrap.md file
Does not edit files unless the user explicitly requests changes
Cannot resolve authority from filenames alone without explicit spec definitions
Output quality depends on the completeness of existing spec documentation

use_cases.title: Audit spec-to-code coverage
use_cases.description: Run this skill to find specs that have no corresponding tests or implementation files, identifying gaps before a release.

use_cases.title: Understand a new SpecDD project
use_cases.description: Use the skill to walk the spec chain and learn how bootstrap rules, ancestor specs, and local specs connect to the codebase.

use_cases.title: Review changed files against specs
use_cases.description: When a pull request modifies files, use this skill to verify each change is authorized by a governing specification.

prompt_templates.title: Basic coverage check
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace the spec chain for the current project. Identify any specs that have no corresponding code, tests, or documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Trace a specific feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace the spec chain for the authentication module. Show which bootstrap rules, ancestor specs, and local specs govern it, and list the implementing files.

prompt_templates.title: Review changed files
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is the diff from the latest commit. For each changed file, find the governing spec that authorizes the change. Flag any unauthorized modifications.

prompt_templates.title: Full project audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk the entire spec chain from .specdd/bootstrap.md to all local specs. Produce a report of missing test coverage, stale specs, and unclear authority chains.

output_examples.input: Trace coverage for the payment processing module
output_examples.output:
Bootstrap: .specdd/bootstrap.md (active)
Ancestor specs: .specdd/specs/system.md
Local spec: src/payments/spec.md (write authority granted)
Implementing files: src/payments/processor.ts, src/payments/validator.ts
Test files: tests/payments/processor.test.ts
Gaps: No test for refund flow; spec.md mentions timeout handling but no implementation found

output_examples.input: What specs authorize the change in src/api/handler.ts?
output_examples.output:
Changed file: src/api/handler.ts
Governing spec: src/api/spec.md (section 3.2: Request validation)
Authorization: Granted — local spec explicitly covers request validation logic
Status: Authorized change

best_practices: Read the bootstrap chain in the exact order specified to avoid missing higher-priority rules
Only re-read bootstrap files when the project root changes or a file may have been modified
Identify the nearest local spec that grants write authority before suggesting any edits
anti_patterns: Do not infer write authority from filenames, symbols, or module conventions alone
Do not re-read the entire spec chain just because a workflow phase changed
Do not use this skill on projects that do not have a .specdd/bootstrap.md file

faq.question: What is SpecDD?
faq.answer: SpecDD is a specification-driven development methodology where project rules live in bootstrap files and specs define contracts that code must satisfy.

faq.question: Can this skill edit my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads files and reports findings. It will not edit code unless you explicitly ask for changes.

faq.question: What if my project does not have a .specdd/bootstrap.md?
faq.answer: This skill will stop and tell you it is not applicable. Use the specdd-adopt skill if you want to add SpecDD to your project.

faq.question: Does this skill run any shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a set of instructions for the AI agent. It does not execute code or make network requests.

faq.question: How does it handle multiple bootstrap files?
faq.answer: The skill reads bootstrap.md first, then bootstrap.project.md if it exists, then bootstrap.local.md if it exists. Later files can narrow earlier rules but cannot silently weaken contracts.

faq.question: What is the output format?
faq.answer: The skill produces a report listing each item with its reason for inclusion, active constraints, satisfying code or tests, and any missing links or gaps.
